meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

source_tables.name: 14-10-0416-01 Labour force characteristics by occupation, annual

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
family.definition: This major group comprises administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations, including office support and data entry occupations; financial, insurance and related administrative support workers; library, correspondence and other clerks, and supply chain logistics, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 4. Occupations classified in TEER 4, as those included in this group with a 4 as the second digit of their code, typically require completion of secondary school; or several weeks of on-the-job training with some secondary school education; or experience in a related occupation from TEER 5, when applicable (TEER 5 includes occupations with a 5 as a second digit in their code).
family.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

units.title: General office support workers
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Office, court, and data support occupations
units.minor_group_title: Office support and court services occupations
units.definition: General office support workers prepare correspondence, reports, statements and other material, operate office equipment, answer telephones, verify, record and process forms and documents such as contracts and requisitions and perform general clerical duties according to established procedures. They are employed in offices throughout the public and private sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Office support and co-ordination occupations (14, 15).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: General office support workers prepare correspondence, reports, statements and other material, operate office equipment, answer telephones and perform clerical duties of a general nature according to established procedures.
main_duties: Respond to telephone, in person or electronic enquiries or forward to appropriate person
Prepare correspondence, reports, statements, forms, presentations, applications and other documents
Process incoming and outgoing mail, manually or electronically
Photocopy and collate documents for distribution, mailing and filing
Send and receive messages and documents using fax machine or electronic mail
Maintain inventory of office supplies, order supplies as required and arrange for servicing of office equipment
Assist in preparing meeting agendas, attend meetings, and record minutes
Assist with administrative procedures such as budget submissions, contracts administration and work schedules
May sort, process and verify receipts, expenditures, forms and other documents
May organize the flow of work for other office support workers
May perform basic bookkeeping tasks such as preparing invoices and bank deposits.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory or office management positions is possible with experience.
Progression to officer level positions is possible with additional training and experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

example_titles.job_title_text: program and service delivery clerk - government services

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: File clerks organize papers, records, documents and other material according to subject matter or other filing system.
main_duties: Scan, sort and file documents according to established guidelines
Locate, retrieve, or make copies of documents from files as requested and maintain records of filed and removed materials
Track documents removed from files to ensure that loan out documents are returned.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school or college business or commercial courses is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory or office management positions is possible with experience.
Progression to officer level positions is possible with additional training and experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

units.title: Receptionists
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Office, court, and data support occupations
units.minor_group_title: Office support and court services occupations
units.definition: Receptionists greet people arriving at offices, hospitals and other establishments, direct visitors to appropriate person or service, answer and forward telephone calls, take messages, schedule appointments and perform other clerical duties. They are employed by hospitals, medical and dental offices and other offices throughout the public and private sectors. Telephone operators are included in this group.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Office support and co-ordination occupations (14, 15).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Receptionists greet people arriving at offices, hospitals and other establishments, direct visitors to appropriate persons and services, answer and forward telephone calls, take messages, schedule appointments and perform other clerical duties.
main_duties: Greet people coming into offices and other establishments, direct them to the appropriate contacts or services, provide general information in person and by phone, and may perform clerical duties and maintain front desk security and security access lists
Schedule appointments, using manual or computerized systems
Record bookings, handle credit card checks, receive payment, issue receipts.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
On-the-job training may be provided.
additional_information: Some receptionists are required to be bilingual or multilingual to perform their duties.
Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Offices throughout the private and public sectors

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Hospital admitting clerks greet people arriving at hospitals and obtain information to direct them to medical service areas.
main_duties: Interview patients to obtain and process information required to provide hospital and medical services
Greet patients, schedule appointments, using manual or computerized systems, receive and record payment for services, and direct patients to appropriate areas.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
On-the-job training may be provided.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Switchboard and answering service operators operate a telephone system or switchboard to answer, screen and forward telephone calls and provide information.
main_duties: Operate a telephone system or switchboard to answer, screen and forward telephone calls, take messages and provide information as required, and may perform clerical duties
Answer telephones and record and relay messages.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
On-the-job training may be provided.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Offices throughout the private and public sectors

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Telephone operators operate telephone systems to advance and complete customers’ telephone calls.
main_duties: Operate telephone systems to advance and complete customers' telephone calls such as long distance, pay telephone, mobile radio/telephone and person to person calls, arrange teleconferences, provide relay phone service to persons with disabilities, and may calculate and record billing information.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
On-the-job training may be provided.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.

units.title: Personnel clerks
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Office, court, and data support occupations
units.minor_group_title: Office support and court services occupations
units.definition: Personnel clerks assist personnel officers and human resources specialists and compile, maintain and process information relating to staffing, recruitment, training, labour relations, performance evaluations and classifications. They are employed in personnel departments throughout the private and public sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Personnel clerks assist personnel officers and human resources specialists and compile, maintain and process information relating to staffing, recruitment, training, labour relations, performance evaluations and classifications
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of college or other courses in personnel administration may be required.
Some clerical experience may be required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.
Progression to officer level positions is possible with additional training and experience.
workplaces_employers: Personnel departments throughout the private and public sectors

example_titles.job_title_text: human resources clerk - Canadian armed forces

core_competencies.statement: to assess and address the emotional and social cues of employees and candidates, facilitating better communication and effective problem resolution
to interpret spoken instructions, inquiries and conversations, especially in contexts like recruitment, training coordination and staff and public questions
to convey information accurately while conducting interviews, explaining policies or addressing queries
to review and process materials, such as documents, forms and detailed records, ensuring information is correct and complete
to ensure all data is entered correctly, discrepancies are caught early and all documentation complies with regulations and policies
to understand clerical duties involved in managing HR functions, such as organizing and filing documents, handling correspondence and maintaining records

units.title: Court clerks and related court services occupations
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Office, court, and data support occupations
units.minor_group_title: Office support and court services occupations
units.definition: Court clerks and workers in related court services occupations perform administrative, security, and other support functions for courts of law such as scheduling trials, processing service fees, calling court to order, preparing court dockets and maintaining exhibits, recording trial proceedings and maintaining court records. They also facilitate court security and public safety, liaise with law enforcement organizations, and provide support to the judiciary. They are employed by federal, provincial, territorial and municipal courts and by police forces.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Office support and co-ordination occupations (14, 15).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Court clerks and related court services occupations
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Court clerks and related court services occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Court clerks and workers in related court services occupations perform administrative, security, and other support functions for courts of law such as scheduling trials, processing service fees, calling court to order, preparing court dockets and maintaining exhibits, recording trial proceedings and maintaining  court records. They also facilitate court security and public safety, liaise with law enforcement organizations, and provide support to the judiciary.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
On-the-job training is provided for court officers, court clerks, and court liaison officers.
Special Constable Certification may be required for court officers and court liaison officers.
Court clerks and court officers may require a 1-2 year post-secondary diploma in office administration or equivalent.
Court administrators usually require a 1-2 year post-secondary diploma, or may require a university degree in law, business administration, judicial administration, or related field.
Some experience as a court clerks may be required for Court administrators.
Experience in clerical services, administration and management, and personnel information systems may be required for court administrators.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory or senior court service positions is possible with experience.
Duties of court administrators may include those of court clerks or court officers depending on the location and size of the courthouse.

core_competencies.statement: to understand and respond to verbal instructions, questions and information, ensuring smooth communication and effective support during court proceedings
to prepare court dockets, record trial proceedings, draft legal documents and maintain detailed records
to manage various tasks simultaneously, such as scheduling trials, processing service fees and maintaining records, while allocating sufficient time to each
to focus on specific details while managing multiple tasks simultaneously, such as scheduling trials, recording proceedings and maintaining records
to review, prepare and manage a variety of documents, including court dockets, evidence and records
to ensure accuracy in all aspects of court administration, from recording trial proceedings to preparing legal documents and maintaining court records
to understand court processes, the context of legal documents and how to interact appropriately with legal professionals

units.title: Survey interviewers and statistical clerks
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Office, court, and data support occupations
units.minor_group_title: Survey, statistical and data entry occupations
units.definition: Survey interviewers contact individuals to gather information for market research, public opinion polls or election and census enumeration. Statistical clerks code and compile interview and other data into reports, lists, directories and other documents. They are employed by market research and polling firms, government departments and agencies, utility companies, contact centres and other establishments. This unit group also includes clerks who observe and record information on traffic flow.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Office support and co-ordination occupations (14, 15).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Survey interviewers contact individuals to gather information for market research, public opinion polls or election and census enumeration.
main_duties: Contact individuals by telephone or in person and explain the purpose of the interview
Ask questions following the outlines of questionnaires and surveys
Record responses on paper or enter responses directly into a computer database through computer-assisted interviewing systems.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required.
Experience in the operation of a computer may be required.
May require driver's licence.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Statistical clerks code and compile interview and other data into reports, lists, directories and other documents.
main_duties: Check information gathered for completeness and accuracy
Code information according to established coding manuals and enter data into statistical-based computer programs
Conduct routine statistical analysis of data
Compile interview and other data into reports and lists.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required.
On-the-job training is usually provided.

units.title: Data entry clerks
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Office, court, and data support occupations
units.minor_group_title: Survey, statistical and data entry occupations
units.definition: Data entry clerks input coded, statistical, financial and other information into computerized databases, spreadsheets or other templates using a keyboard, mouse, or optical scanner, speech recognition software or other data entry tools. They are employed in the private and public sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Office support and co-ordination occupations (14, 15).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Data entry clerks input coded, statistical, financial and other information into computerized databases, spreadsheets or other templates using a keyboard, mouse, or optical scanner, speech recognition software or other data entry tools.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required.
College or other courses in data entry may be required.
additional_information: Progression to senior positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

core_competencies.statement: to interpret written instructions or data from various sources, ensuring an accurate understanding of the data to enter
to handle workload efficiently, meet deadlines and manage multiple data entry tasks
to ensure data is entered into the correct fields and follows the intended structure of databases or spreadsheets
to focus on text and numeric fields during data entry, ensuring precision when handling large volumes of information across multiple software systems
to identify and correct mistakes or inaccuracies within databases or spreadsheets
to understand how to perform administrative tasks that encompass data entry work, such as organizing documents, managing data records and handling correspondence

units.title: Desktop publishing operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Office, court, and data support occupations
units.minor_group_title: Survey, statistical and data entry occupations
units.definition: Desktop publishing operators and workers in related occupations use computers to enter copy into a typesetting system or operate typesetting output equipment to produce text that is ready to print. They are employed by firms that specialize in typesetting, commercial printing companies, publishing and printing companies and various establishments in the public and private sectors that have in-house printing departments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Desktop publishing operators and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Desktop publishing operators operate desktop publishing software and equipment to design, lay out and produce copy ready to be print.
main_duties: Operate desktop publishing software and equipment to design, lay out and produce camera-ready copy and may perform pre-flight operator duties
Mark copy with computer codes and typographic instructions before it is typeset.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a college program in graphic arts, or other training in typography, computer typesetting or desktop publishing, is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible through experience.
workplaces_employers: In-house printing departments of private and public sector establishments

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Desktop publishing operators and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Typesetting input and output operators use computers to enter copy into a typesetting system or operate typesetting output equipment to produce text that is ready to print.
main_duties: Input copy, codes or commands and subsequent corrections into typesetting device or system
Operate media conversion equipment to process and format copy transmitted through telecommunications
Operate and tend typesetting output devices, load and process photosensitive material and perform routine maintenance.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a college program in graphic arts, or other training in typography, computer typesetting or desktop publishing, is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible through experience.
workplaces_employers: In-house printing departments of private and public sector establishments

units.title: Accounting and related clerks
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Financial, insurance and related administrative support workers
units.minor_group_title: Financial, insurance and related administrative support workers
units.definition: Accounting and related clerks calculate, prepare and process bills, invoices, accounts payable and receivable, budgets and other financial records according to established procedures. They are employed throughout the private and public sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Accounting and related clerks calculate, prepare and process bills, invoices, accounts payable and receivable, budgets and other financial records according to established procedures.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of college or other courses certified by the Certified General Accountants Association of Canada, Canadian Securities Institute or Canadian Bookkeepers Association may be required.
Some accounting clerks must be bondable.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory or skilled financial occupations, such as accounts payable supervisor, bookkeeper or office manager is possible with additional training and experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

core_competencies.statement: to interpret spoken instructions, inquiries and explanations from clients, colleagues and supervisors, ensuring correct processing of financial transactions and proper handling of account-related tasks
to identify the root causes of financial problems, develop solutions and implement corrective actions
to ensure financial data is processed correctly and accomplish tasks such as preparing invoices, processing bills and managing budgets
to ensure financial records, invoices and other documents are filed and processed in an orderly manner
to catch mistakes, verify accuracy and maintain the integrity of financial records
to understand how to prepare accurate financial statements, manage accounts payable and receivable and ensure compliance with financial policies and procedures

units.title: Banking, insurance and other financial clerks
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Financial, insurance and related administrative support workers
units.minor_group_title: Financial, insurance and related administrative support workers
units.definition: Banking, insurance and other financial clerks compile, process and maintain banking, insurance and other financial information. They are employed by banks, credit companies, private and public insurance establishments, investment firms and other financial establishments throughout the private and public sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Banking, insurance and other financial clerks
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Bank clerks compile, process and maintain banking and other financial information.
main_duties: Compile records of deposits, withdrawals, loan and mortgage payments, cheques and purchase, sale and exchange of securities
Process loan and mortgage applications, loan and mortgage payments, retirement savings plan applications, term deposits, drafts and money orders

Verify and balance automatic teller machine transactions and ledger entries, calculate service charges and interest payments and notify customers regarding account discrepancies and captured bank cards
Answer enquiries and provide information on banking products, policies and services
May sell drafts, money orders, travellers' cheques and foreign currency, rent safety deposit boxes and open and close savings, chequing and other accounts.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A business college diploma may be required.
On-the-job training and short-term training courses or programs specific to the financial establishment are usually required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Banking, insurance and other financial clerks
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Insurance clerks compile, process and maintain insurance information.
main_duties: Process enrolments, cancellations, claims transactions, policy changes and premium payments
Review insurance applications and verify insurance coverage, premiums paid and other insurance information
Calculate insurance premiums, pension benefits and annuity payments
Compile and maintain claims data, rates and insurance data and records
Answer enquiries and provide information on insurance products, policies and services.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A business college diploma may be required.
On-the-job training and short-term training courses or programs specific to the financial establishment are usually required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Financial establishments throughout the private and public sectors

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Banking, insurance and other financial clerks
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Financial clerks compile, process and maintain financial information.
main_duties: Compile and maintain rental, sale and other real estate listings
Compile and maintain stock, bond and other securities listings
Sort, verify and process real estate, securities and other financial transactions
Answer enquiries and reply to correspondence.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A business college diploma may be required.
On-the-job training and short-term training courses or programs specific to the financial establishment are usually required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Financial establishments throughout the private and public sectors

units.title: Collection clerks
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Financial, insurance and related administrative support workers
units.minor_group_title: Financial, insurance and related administrative support workers
units.definition: Collection clerks collect payments on overdue accounts and bad cheques and locate debtors to make collection arrangements. They are employed by collection agencies, utility companies, department stores, loan companies, banks and credit unions, and by financial and licensing departments within governments.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Collection clerks collect payments on overdue accounts and bad cheques and locate debtors to make collection arrangements.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A business college diploma may be required.
A period of on-the-job training is often provided for collection clerks and collection officers.
A collections licence issued by the province or territory of employment is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to senior or supervisory positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Financial and licensing departments within governments

example_titles.job_title_text: inspector and bill collector - electric power company
inspector and bill collector - gas company

core_competencies.statement: to negotiate payment terms and persuade debtors to make payments or enter into payment plans
to better understand debtor concerns and motivations, tailor their approach to different individuals and manage interactions in a way that is more likely to result in successful collections
to effectively communicate with debtors, clearly explaining the terms of the debt, addressing concerns and providing information about payment options
to quickly recognize trends or patterns in debt collection data, such as common reasons for overdue payments or frequent issues with specific accounts
to accurately track payments, update account records and ensure all details of agreements and communications are properly documented
to help manage interactions with debtors in a professional and empathetic manner, providing a positive experience even in difficult situations

units.title: Library assistants and clerks
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.definition: Library assistants and clerks issue and receive library materials, sort and shelve books and provide general library information to users. They also perform clerical functions. Library clerks are employed by libraries or other establishments with library services.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Library assistants and clerks issue and receive library materials, sort and shelve books and provide general library information to users. They also perform clerical functions.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to more senior positions is possible with experience and post-secondary education related to library science.
workplaces_employers: Establishments with library services throughout the public and private sectors

example_titles.job_title_text: braille and talking books library clerk

core_competencies.statement: to manage the flow of materials, including issuing and receiving items, tracking their status and maintaining accurate inventory
to manage the circulation of materials, schedule events or programs and ensure tasks are completed in an orderly manner
to interpret written materials, which is essential for processing library records, understanding cataloguing systems and following procedures outlined in library policies
to read labels, book titles and library cards as well as to sort and shelve books
to clearly articulate the library’s services, policies and procedures to users, helping them understand how to borrow materials, use resources and participate in programs
to collaborate with other staff members in order to manage library programs, handle user inquiries and solve operational issues
to handle user requests, provide accurate information and assist with library services in a courteous and professional manner

units.title: Correspondence, publication and regulatory clerks
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.definition: Correspondence, publication and regulatory clerks write correspondence, proofread material for accuracy, compile material for publication, verify, record and process forms and documents, such as applications, licences, permits, contracts, registrations and requisitions, and perform other related clerical duties in accordance with established procedures, guidelines and schedules. They are employed by newspapers, periodicals, publishing firms and by establishments throughout the private and public sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Classified advertising clerks take orders, calculate rates, bill customers for classified advertisements, write and edit ad copy and perform other related clerical duties.
main_duties: Receive customers' orders for classified advertising, write and edit copy, calculate advertising costs and bill customers.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Additional courses or a diploma in writing, journalism or a related field may be required.
Previous clerical or administrative experience may be required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Correspondence clerks write correspondence, proofread material for accuracy and perform other related clerical duties in accordance with established procedures, guidelines and schedules.
main_duties: Write business and government correspondence such as replies to requests for information and assistance, damage claims, credit and billing enquiries and service complaints.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Additional courses or a diploma in writing, journalism or a related field may be required.
Previous clerical or administrative experience may be required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Proofreaders and editorial assistants assist in the preparation of periodicals, advertisements, catalogues, directories and other material for publication by conducting research, verifying facts and detecting grammatical, typographical or compositional errors.
main_duties: Assist in the preparation of periodicals, advertisements, catalogues, directories and other material for publication
Verify facts and conduct research
Read material prior to publication to detect and mark for correction any grammatical, typographical or compositional errors.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Additional courses or a diploma in writing, journalism or a related field may be required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Readers and press clippers read newspapers, magazines, press releases and other publications to locate, file and distribute articles and to perform other related clerical duties in accordance with established procedures, guidelines and schedules.
main_duties: Read newspapers, magazines, press releases and other publications to locate and file articles of interest to staff and clients.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Additional courses or a diploma in writing, journalism or a related field may be required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Regulatory clerks compile, verify, record and process applications, licences, permits, contracts, registrations, requisitions and other forms and documents in accordance with established procedures using processing systems; and authorize and issue licences, permits, registration papers, reimbursements and other material after requested documents have been processed and approved.
main_duties: Compile, verify, record and process applications, licences, permits, contracts, registrations, requisitions and other forms and documents in accordance with established procedures using processing systems
Authorize and issue licences, permits, registration papers, reimbursements and other material after requested documents have been processed and approved.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Previous clerical or administrative experience may be required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

units.title: Shippers and receivers
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Supply chain logistics, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations
units.minor_group_title: Supply chain logistics, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations
units.definition: Shippers and receivers ship, receive and record the movement of parts, supplies, materials, equipment and stock to and from an establishment. They are employed in the public sector and by retail and wholesale establishments, manufacturing companies, and other commercial and industrial establishments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Shippers and receivers ship, receive and record the movement of parts, supplies, materials, equipment and stock to and from an establishment.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Experience in a related clerical occupation or as a warehouse worker may be required.
A forklift certificate may be required.
A driver's licence may be required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with additional training or experience.

example_titles.job_title_text: milk and cream receiver - food and beverage processing

core_competencies.statement: to synchronize the arrival and dispatch of goods, coordinating with other departments or external partners and manage the flow of materials to prevent bottlenecks
to oversee shipment progress, check for any issues such as delays or discrepancies and ensure all materials are accounted for and handled according to procedures

to monitor surrounding activity while focusing on core tasks, detecting movement in the periphery to prevent accidents and ensure efficient handling of shipments in busy warehouse or docking environments
to repeatedly lift, carry and move heavy packages throughout the workday, maintaining muscular endurance to handle physically demanding tasks
to meticulously check and record details such as item numbers, quantities and conditions of goods
to understand how to document and process shipping and receiving transactions, manage inventory records and ensure compliance with procedural guidelines

units.title: Storekeepers and partspersons
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Supply chain logistics, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations
units.minor_group_title: Supply chain logistics, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations
units.definition: Storekeepers and partspersons sort, store and issue parts and supplies for use by the establishment in which they work and for sale to the public. They are employed by manufacturing companies, warehouses, retail and wholesale establishments, mining, forestry and construction companies, repair shops, hospitals and other establishments. Apprentices are also included in this unit group.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Storekeepers and partspersons sort, store and issue parts and supplies for use by the establishment in which they work and for sale to the public. Apprentices are also included in this unit group.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of a three-year apprenticeship program orcompletion of specialized training in high school and a one-year college program for partspersons may be required.
Trade certification for partspersons is available, but voluntary, in all provinces and territories.
Experience in a related clerical occupation, or as a warehouse worker, may be required for partspersons.
Red Seal endorsement is also available to qualified partspersons upon successful completion of the interprovincial Red Seal examination.
additional_information: The Red Seal endorsement allows for interprovincial mobility.
Progression to supervisory positions is possible with additional training or experience.

Partspersons may specialize in a particular line of parts such as automotive parts, recreational vehicle parts, marine parts, heavy equipment parts, agricultural machinery parts, heating, ventilating and air conditioning parts or refrigeration parts.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

example_titles.job_title_text: industrial engines and equipment partsperson - retail
automobile parts counter sales clerk - retail

core_competencies.statement: to maintain appropriate stock levels, reduce waste and ensure the establishment can meet customers needs without overstocking or running out of critical items
to interact with customers, especially when selling products or explaining features and benefits of parts or supplies, in a way that increases sales and customer satisfaction
to recognize social cues and understand the context of interactions, helping to communicate more effectively with customers, colleagues and suppliers
to stock shelves, organize inventory and handle deliveries safely and efficiently
to sustain intense physical activity throughout the day, including lifting, carrying, reaching, bending and walking in warehouse or retail settings
to avoid errors in inventory management, ensure correct parts are issued and maintain accurate records
to understand how to manage the flow of goods and materials from suppliers to customers

units.title: Production logistics workers
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Supply chain logistics, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations
units.minor_group_title: Supply chain logistics, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations
units.definition: Production logistics workers expedite the flow of work and materials within an establishment and prepare merchandise ready for shipment. They are employed by manufacturing and construction companies, printing and publishing companies and other industrial establishments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Production logistics workers expedite the flow of work and materials within an establishment and prepare merchandise ready for shipment.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to coordination positions is possible with experience.

core_competencies.statement: to prioritize tasks, manage workload and ensure shipment deadlines are meet
to identify issues, analyze potential solutions and solve problems that may arise in the logistics process
to manage communication between different departments, align schedules and ensure materials and resources are available when needed
to organize inventory, schedule shipments and manage production processes
to read and verify product details and ensure merchandise is prepared correctly for shipment
to ensure all aspects of the shipping process, from receiving materials to preparing orders, are handled correctly, avoiding mistakes in shipments, labeling or inventory
to understand the workflows dynamic and improve the overall production and shipping processes

units.title: Purchasing and inventory control workers
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Supply chain logistics, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations
units.minor_group_title: Supply chain logistics, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations
units.definition: Purchasing and inventory control workers process purchasing transactions and maintain inventories of materials, equipment and stock. They are employed by retail and wholesale establishments, manufacturing companies, government agencies and other establishments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Office support and co-ordination occupations (14, 15).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Purchasing control workers process purchasing transactions, resolve supply problems and prepare and maintain files, reports and price lists.
main_duties: Review requisition orders for accuracy and verify that materials, equipment and stock are not available from current inventories
Source and obtain price quotations from catalogues and suppliers and prepare purchase orders
Calculate cost of orders and charge or forward invoices to appropriate accounts
Process purchases within purchasing authority
Contact suppliers to schedule deliveries and to resolve shortages, missed deliveries and other problems
Prepare and maintain purchasing files, reports and price lists.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Courses in purchasing management may be required for purchasing control workers.
additional_information: The duties of purchasing and inventory control workers may overlap in some establishments.
Progression to supervisory positions or more senior occupations such as purchasing agent or retail buyer is possible with additional training or experience.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Inventory control workers compile and maintain inventories of materials, equipment and stock, and monitor inventory levels.
main_duties: Monitor inventory levels as materials, equipment and stock are issued, transferred within an establishment or sold to the public using manual or computerized inventory systems
Compile inventory reports, recording the quantity, type and value of materials, equipment and stock on hand, using manual or computerized inventory systems
Prepare requisition orders to replenish materials, equipment and stock
Maintain stock rotation and dispose of and account for outdated stock
Enter data for production scheduling, stock replenishment/relocation and inventory adjustments
Reconcile physical inventories with computer counts.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Courses in production and inventory management and ability to operate a computerized inventory system may be required for inventory control workers.
additional_information: The duties of purchasing and inventory control workers may overlap in some establishments.
Progression to supervisory positions or more senior occupations such as purchasing agent or retail buyer is possible with additional training or experience.

units.title: Dispatchers
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Supply chain logistics, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations
units.minor_group_title: Supply chain logistics, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations
units.definition: Dispatchers operate radios and other telecommunication equipment to dispatch emergency vehicles and to coordinate the activities of drivers and other personnel. They are employed by police, fire and health departments, other emergency service agencies, taxi, delivery and courier services, trucking and utilities companies, and other commercial and industrial establishments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Dispatchers operate radios and other telecommunication equipment to dispatch emergency vehicles and to coordinate the activities of drivers and other personnel. They are employed by police, fire and health departments, other emergency service agencies, taxi, delivery and courier services, trucking and utilities companies, and other commercial and industrial establishments.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Police and emergency dispatchers and other radio operators usually require provincial radio operator's certificates.
Police and emergency dispatchers are required to complete formal on-the-job training. Other dispatchers usually undergo some informal on-the-job training.

core_competencies.statement: to allocate resources efficiently so responses are timely and all parties involved, such as personnel and vehicles, are working in concert
to continuously observe and assess real-time information, such as vehicles and personnel statuses, to respond to changing conditions, identify issues and provide updates
to concentrate on urgent messages or critical information without being overwhelmed by less important details, ensuring effective and timely responses
to accurately understand and interpret verbal information from callers, drivers and emergency personnel
to ensure all information, such as locations, vehicle statuses and instructions, is recorded and communicated accurately
to better allocate vehicles and personnel based on current needs and priorities

units.title: Transportation route and crew schedulers
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Supply chain logistics, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations
units.minor_group_title: Supply chain logistics, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations
units.definition: Transportation route and crew schedulers prepare operational and crew schedules for transportation equipment and operating personnel. They are employed by municipal transit commissions, truck, delivery and courier companies, railways, airlines and by other transportation establishments in both the private and public sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Transportation route and crew schedulers prepare operational and crew schedules for transportation equipment and operating personnel.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Several years of experience in an appropriate transportation sector are usually required.
On-the-job training may be provided.
additional_information: Mobility between occupations in this group is limited due to the requirement of specific transportation sector experience.
Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Transportation establishments in both the private and public sectors

core_competencies.statement: to ensure all elements of transportation operations work together efficiently, including synchronizing crew schedules with transportation routes, managing vehicle assignments and making sure that resources are allocated effectively
to identify and address any issues that arise, such as delays or conflicts, and make adjustments as needed to ensure smooth and efficient operations
to handle several tasks simultaneously, such as managing multiple schedules, addressing conflicts and responding to changes or requests
to organize and manage schedules, ensuring all relevant details, such as crew assignments, vehicle availability and route planning, are properly arranged and easily accessible
to remain focused, make sound decisions and maintain a positive and productive working environment even under challenging conditions
to plan routes that minimize travel time, reduce costs and improve overall efficiency
